title: The Story We Never Told
content: People who met us separately never knew there had once been an 'us.' They knew different versions of our lives, shaped by different cities, careers, and friendships. Our story remained hidden inside old photographs, forgotten train tickets, and memories that belonged only to the two of us. Sometimes I wondered whether a love that no one else witnessed became less real with time. Then I remembered the countless ordinary afternoons we spent talking about books, cooking dinner together, or watching the rain from the window. Those moments never needed an audience to matter. Not every meaningful story is shared with the world. Some exist quietly within the people who lived them, changing them forever without ever becoming part of anyone else's memories.
